l.hint: Loads a saved configuration file so you don't have to set everything up again.

l.label: Lora
l.hint: A small add-on file that teaches the AI a specific style, character, or object.

l.label: Local
l.hint: AI models that are already saved on your hard drive and ready to go.

l.label: LTX
l.hint: Short for the LTX video generation toolset.

l.label: Latent Corrections
l.hint: Fixes or adjustments made 'behind the scenes' while the AI is thinking.

l.label: Layerwise Casting
l.hint: A technical setting to optimize how the AI processes data to save memory.

l.label: LinFusion
l.hint: A specific method for faster image generation.

l.label: Log Display
l.hint: Shows the system's text history and background activity.

l.label: List all locally available models
l.hint: Shows a full list of every model currently saved on your computer.

l.label: Last Generate
l.hint: Settings or info related to the very last image you created.

l.label: LUT
l.hint: A color filter (Look-Up Table) used to change the colors of your image professionally. Upload a .cube file to apply it.

l.label: low order
l.hint: A setting to prioritize or simplify the calculation sequence.

l.label: LSC layer indices
l.hint: Specific 'layers' or steps within the AI model to target for adjustments.

l.label: LSC fully qualified name
l.hint: The exact technical name of the AI component being adjusted.

l.label: LSC skip attention blocks
l.hint: Tell the AI to ignore specific 'attention' parts of the math to see how it changes the image.

l.label: LSC skip feed-forward blocks
l.hint: Tell the AI to skip certain internal calculation steps.

l.label: LSC skip attention scores
l.hint: Skip the math that calculates where the AI focuses its attention.

l.label: LSC dropout rate
l.hint: Adjusts how much random data is ignored to prevent the AI from getting 'stuck' or over-focused.

l.label: LUT strength
l.hint: Controls how intense the color filter is. 1.0 is normal, lower is faint, higher is very strong.

l.label: Latent brightness
l.hint: Change the brightness of the image while the AI is still building the initial 'latent' shape.

l.label: Latent sharpen
l.hint: Adjust how sharp or blurry the image is while it is being generated.

l.label: Latent color
l.hint: Adjust the overall color balance of the image while it is still being created.

l.label: Latent clamp
l.hint: Helps fix weird artifacts by limiting how 'crazy' the math values get during the creation process.

l.label: Latent range
l.hint: Set the boundaries for the math values used to build the image.

l.label: Latent threshold
l.hint: A limit to define when the math should 'cap off' to prevent distortions.

l.label: Latent maximize
l.hint: Boosts the dynamic range (contrast/color) of the raw data to make it look better in editing programs.

l.label: Latent center
l.hint: Adjusts the center point of the image math to shift the overall tone.

l.label: Latent max range
l.hint: Sets the highest allowed limit for the math values during creation.

l.label: Latent tint
l.hint: Adds a specific color tint to the image during generation.

l.label: Layer options
l.hint: Advanced settings to manually tweak how the AI layers its work (specifically for IP Adapters).

l.label: Layer scales
l.hint: Controls the influence of different AI layers on the final image.

l.label: Length
l.hint: Defines the length or duration of the effect.

l.label: Loops
l.hint: How many times the AI should re-process the image. Each loop takes the previous result and improves it.

l.label: Level
l.hint: Adjusts the intensity or hierarchical level of the operation.

l.label: Latent mode
l.hint: Tells the video tool to work in the latent (math) space rather than the final pixel space.

l.label: Loop video
l.hint: Enables the video to play in a continuous repeat cycle.

l.label: LLM model
l.hint: Select the AI brain used to rewrite or improve your prompts. (Icons show if it 'sees' images or 'thinks' deeply).

l.label: LBM Method
l.hint: The technical method used for LBM processing.

l.label: LBM Composite
l.hint: Settings for blending multiple parts together.

l.label: LBM Steps
l.hint: How many calculation steps to perform for the LBM process.

l.label: left
l.hint: Operation setting for the left side of the image.

l.label: Live update
l.hint: Allows the preview or settings to update in real-time.

l.label: Low threshold
l.hint: The minimum level required for an action to trigger.

l.label: Large
l.hint: Sets the operation size to large.

l.label: LTX model
l.hint: Pick the specific LTX AI model you want to use for video.

l.label: LTX frames number
l.hint: Decide how many individual video frames to create.

l.label: LTX frames skip
l.hint: Skip specific frames to speed up creation or change the video motion.

l.label: LTX enable upsampling
l.hint: Turns on a feature to make the video higher quality/resolution.

l.label: LTX upsample ratio
l.hint: Controls how much to improve the video resolution.

l.label: LTX enable refine
l.hint: Enables a final pass to sharpen and fix details in the video.

l.label: LTX refine strength
l.hint: How strong the final polish pass should be.

l.label: LTX decode timestep
l.hint: An advanced timing setting for the video decoder.

l.label: LTX enable audio
l.hint: Includes audio track generation for the video.

l.label: Loop
l.hint: Enable a looping action.

l.label: Local directory name
l.hint: Name of the folder on your computer where this extension should be installed.

l.label: Libs
l.hint: Software libraries required for this feature.

l.label: Latent history size
l.hint: How many previous states the system should remember.

l.label: LLama repo
l.hint: The internet location (repository) to download the LLama model from.

l.label: low noise
l.hint: Reduces the amount of 'static' or graininess in the generation.

l.label: Load caption models direct to GPU
l.localized: Load caption models direct to GPU
l.hint: Uses your graphics card's memory instead of your computer's regular RAM to speed up image-to-text features.

l.label: leaf_level
l.hint: A technical setting regarding how deep the AI processes instructions.

l.label: LLM
l.hint: Settings for the Large Language Model.

l.label: Layerwise casting storage
l.hint: Where the system stores temporary data for layerwise calculations.

l.label: Layerwise non-blocking operations
l.hint: A setting to keep the program responsive while it does heavy calculations.

l.label: Lumina: Use mask in transformers
l.hint: Tells the Lumina model to respect the mask you've drawn.

l.label: Listen on all interfaces
l.hint: Allows other computers on your network to access your instance.

l.label: LinFusion apply distillation on load
l.hint: Applies a 'distillation' process (making a model lighter) when it loads.

l.label: Light
l.hint: A lighter theme or simplified mode.

l.label: Log view update period
l.hint: How often the text logs on the screen should refresh, in milliseconds.

l.label: Live preview display period
l.hint: How often (every N steps) to show you the progress image. Set to 0 to turn off.

l.label: Load custom Diffusers pipeline
l.hint: Loads a specialized AI pipeline from HuggingFace.

l.label: LoRA force reload always
l.hint: Makes the AI re-read the LoRA file every time you generate. Useful if you are editing the LoRA file while working.

l.label: LoRA load using Diffusers method
l.hint: Uses the standard Diffusers way to load a LoRA. Might be less compatible but more 'official'.

l.label: LoRA native apply to text encoder
l.localized: LoRA native apply to text encoder
l.hint: Applies the LoRA's influence to the text-reading part of the AI.

l.label: LoRA native fuse with model
l.hint: Permanently combines the LoRA with the main model. Saves memory, but you must reload the model to 'un-fuse' it.

l.label: LoRA diffusers fuse with model
l.hint: Combines the LoRA with the model using the Diffusers method. Same warning as above: reload model to remove.

l.label: LoRA auto-apply tags
l.hint: Automatically adds the special 'trigger words' needed to make the LoRA work to your prompt.

l.label: LoRA memory cache
l.hint: How many LoRAs to keep in your computer's memory so they load instantly next time.

l.label: LoRA add hash info to metadata
l.localized: LoRA add hash info to metadata
l.hint: Writes a digital fingerprint into the image data so you can always check which LoRA was used.

l.label: LDSR Path
l.hint: File path setting for the LDSR upscaler.

l.label: LoRA load using legacy method
l.hint: Uses the older method for loading LoRAs.

l.label: Loaded LoRA
l.hint: Shows the LoRAs currently in use.

l.label: LoRA target filename
l.hint: The file name of the LoRA model.

l.label: Layer skip guidance
l.hint: Adjusts how much the AI focuses on specific layers to guide the generation.

l.label: LineArt
l.hint: A tool that extracts the outlines from an image to use as a guide for generation.

l.label: Leres Depth
l.hint: A tool that calculates the 'depth' (what is close and what is far) of an image.

m.label: Mask

m.label: Model Compile

m.label: Manual Block Merge
m.hint: Merge specific pieces of models for advanced customization.

m.label: Merge Modules
m.hint: Replace or swap parts of a model during the merging process.

m.label: Model
m.hint: The main AI model you are using.

m.label: Model metadata
m.hint: Information about the model like its author and version.

m.label: ModernUI
m.hint: Switch to a cleaner, newer looking interface.

m.label: Modular Pipelines
m.hint: Advanced workflow settings for building custom AI processes.

m.label: Models Paths
m.hint: Where your computer looks to find your downloaded models.

m.label: Mobile
m.hint: Optimize the layout to be easier to use on phones.

m.label: Merge multiple models
m.hint: Blend several models at once.

m.label: Max shift
m.hint: The most the AI can 'shift' the image to improve quality at high resolutions.

m.label: Merge detailers
m.hint: Combine results from different image improvers into one mask before finishing.

m.label: Max detected
m.hint: The maximum number of items the AI will look for to improve.

m.label: Min confidence
m.hint: How sure the AI must be before it decides to fix an object.

m.label: Max overlap
m.hint: How much two items can overlap before the AI ignores one of them.

m.label: Min size
m.hint: The smallest percentage of the picture an object can be to be noticed.

m.label: Max size
m.hint: The largest percentage of the picture an object can be to be noticed.

m.label: Midtones
m.hint: Change the brightness of the middle colors. Positive makes them brighter, negative makes them darker.

m.label: Momentum
m.hint: Controls how much the AI carries over 'speed' from previous steps.

m.label: Mode x-axis
m.hint: Tiling mode for the horizontal direction.

m.label: Mode y-axis
m.hint: Tiling mode for the vertical direction.

m.label: Mask Dropout
m.hint: Randomly ignores parts of the mask for better variety.

m.label: Multi decoder
m.hint: Use multiple decoders to potentially improve speed or quality.

m.label: Mode
m.hint: How to analyze the image: Fast (Quick), Classic (Balanced), Best (High quality), or Negative (For negative prompts).

m.label: Method
m.hint: Choose the technique or algorithm used for this process.

m.label: Model repo
m.hint: The ID of the model on the HuggingFace website.

m.label: Model gguf
m.hint: Optional: A link to a GGUF (a smaller, compressed model format) on HuggingFace.

m.label: Model type
m.hint: Optional: Pick the compression level for the GGUF model.

m.label: Model file
m.hint: Optional: Choose a specific model file inside the downloaded folder.

m.label: Max tokens
m.hint: The maximum word/data count the AI is allowed to write. It will stop immediately once it hits this number.

m.label: masked
m.hint: Only apply changes to the area you painted.

m.label: Mask invert
m.hint: Flip the mask: everything unpainted becomes painted, and vice-versa.

m.label: Mask strength
m.hint: How heavily to apply the mask changes.

m.label: Multistep restore
m.hint: Perform multiple passes to restore image quality.

m.label: Mask blur
m.hint: Smooth out the edges of your mask by this many pixels.

m.label: Min guidance
m.hint: Lower limit for how strictly the AI follows your prompt.

m.label: Max guidance
m.hint: Upper limit for how strictly the AI follows your prompt.

m.label: Motion level
m.hint: How much 'movement' to allow in the video generation.

m.label: Mode after
m.hint: What to do with the mask after the main job is finished.

m.label: Method after
m.hint: Which technique to use after the main job is finished.

m.label: Mode mask
m.hint: How to handle the mask for this specific operation.

m.label: Method mask
m.hint: Which technical method to use for the mask.

m.label: Maximum units
m.hint: The highest number of control units allowed.

m.label: Max faces
m.hint: The most faces the AI will attempt to fix.

m.label: Medium
m.hint: The preferred 'strength' or settings balance.

m.label: Merge alpha
m.hint: Determines the blending ratio when merging.

m.label: Mask only
m.hint: Work exclusively on the masked area.

m.label: Max tags
m.hint: Limit the number of description tags. Keeps only the most confident ones.

m.label: Memory
m.hint: Settings for managing your computer's RAM/VRAM.

m.label: Memory optimization
m.hint: Try to use memory more efficiently to avoid crashing.

m.label: Model Info
m.hint: Details about the currently loaded model.

m.label: Model pipeline
m.hint: If it doesn't recognize your model, pick the type manually here.

m.label: Model auto-load on start
m.hint: Load your default model automatically when you open the program.

m.label: Model load using multiple threads
m.hint: Load the model faster by using more of your CPU's power.

m.label: Model auto-download on demand
m.hint: Download models automatically when they are requested.

m.label: Model load using streams
m.hint: Better for loading models over slow network storage.

m.label: Model load model direct to GPU

m.hint: Load model directly to your graphics card.

m.label: Model types not to offload
m.hint: Keep these models on the graphics card at all times.

m.hint: Skip compressing these model types.

m.label: Modules to not convert
m.hint: Do not change these parts during conversion.

m.label: Modules dtype dict
m.hint: Advanced: Map specific parts to data formats.

m.label: Math
m.hint: Settings for how the GPU handles calculations.

m.label: Memory limit
m.hint: Set a limit on how much memory the program can use.

m.label: migraphx
m.hint: Enable MIGraphX optimization (for specific hardware).

m.label: max-autotune
m.hint: Run the fastest possible tuning for your GPU.

m.label: max-autotune-no-cudagraphs
m.hint: Fast tuning without using CUDA graphs.

m.label: Maximum image size (MP)
m.hint: Cap the resolution of generated images (in Megapixels).

m.label: Max words
m.hint: Limit for the number of words in a saved file.

m.label: Modern
m.hint: Use modern interface styles.

m.label: Mount URL subpath
m.hint: Advanced: Set a specific web path for the server.

m.label: Mobile scale
m.hint: Adjust the zoom level for mobile screens.

m.label: Move detailer model to CPU when complete

m.label: Move base model to CPU when using refiner

m.hint: Swap memory usage to the main computer RAM to save space.

m.label: Move base model to CPU when using VAE
m.localized: Move base model to CPU when using VAE
m.hint: Free up GPU space while the image encoder (VAE) is active.

m.label: Move refiner model to CPU when not in use

m.label: Move VAE and CLIP to RAM when training

m.hint: Free up space on your GPU to allow for larger training runs.

m.label: Model name

m.label: Model base path

m.label: Max shard size
m.hint: Maximum file size for splitting large models.

m.label: Model class

m.label: Mid Block

m.label: Model precision

m.label: Maximum rank

m.label: Midas depth

m.label: MLSD

m.label: MediaPipe Face

m.label: Marigold Depth

n.label: Number
n.hint: A specific numerical value setting.

n.hint: A setting related to the 'What to avoid' prompt.

n.label: None
n.hint: No selection or empty value.

n.label: NSFW allowed
n.hint: Enable this if you want the AI to generate mature or explicit images.

n.label: Noise strength
n.hint: Controls how much 'chaos' or initial randomness affects the image creation process.

n.label: NMS
n.hint: A technical setting that helps group overlapping objects during detection.

n.label: Near threshold
n.hint: How close objects need to be to be considered part of the same group.

n.label: Noise scale
n.hint: Sets the overall intensity of the random noise in the generation process.

n.label: Note
n.hint: A brief message or reminder.

n.hint: Allows the computer to move files in the background without freezing the interface.

n.label: native
n.hint: Using the default, standard method.

n.label: no-grad
n.hint: Tells the computer to stop calculating extra data for training, which saves memory and speeds up image creation.

n.label: Numbered filenames
n.hint: Saves your images as 001.png, 002.png instead of long, confusing names.

n.label: Network card size (px)
n.hint: Changes how big the thumbnail pictures look in your extensions menu.

n.label: Noise multiplier for image processing
n.hint: Increases the effect of noise during image editing or post-processing.

n.label: New model name

n.label: Number of ReBasin Iterations
n.hint: How many times the AI tries to align and blend two different models together to create a smooth mix.

n.label: Network prompt

n.label: Network negative prompt

n.label: Network parameters

o.label: Optional

o.label: OpenVINO

o.label: Other...
o.hint: More miscellaneous settings.

o.hint: Pick a different timing method for the noise reduction process, ignoring the default.

o.label: Order

o.label: Override resolution
o.hint: Force a specific image size, ignoring the settings inside the video file.

o.label: Output directory
o.hint: The specific folder on your computer where your finished images will be saved.

o.label: original
o.hint: The standard, basic way the model was originally built to run.

o.hint: Move the tools that write image descriptions to your computer's main memory to free up your graphics card.

o.hint: Free up graphics memory during the setup phase of the process.

o.hint: Use data streaming to move models out of the graphics card memory more efficiently.

o.hint: The maximum amount of memory your graphics card can hold before needing to offload data.

o.label: OpenVINO activations mode
o.hint: How the model calculates its internal signals using OpenVINO.

o.hint: Choose which hardware (like your graphics card) should be used to run the ONNX model.

o.hint: If your graphics card fails to run the task, let your computer's main processor (CPU) finish the work instead.

o.hint: Use 16-bit math for better speed. If unchecked, it uses 32-bit, which is slower but more precise.

o.label: Olive use static dimensions
o.hint: Make the model faster by locking it to one specific image size.

o.hint: Keep a copy of the optimized models so they load faster later.

o.hint: Turn off the feature that saves the compiled model to your hard drive.

o.hint: Don't clear temporary memory once the model is ready to run.

o.label: olive-ai

o.label: openvino_fx

o.label: OpenBody
